title: Nocturne Sessions duotone gig poster: two-tone artist-photo FX + wordmark vectorize + InDesign template merge to print PDF/JPEG
grounding_note: VERBATIM from the Freelancer.com "Modern Album Logo & Cover Design" brief: the client is "getting ready to release a new project and need a striking album cover that also doubles as a recognisable logo for all my promo materials"; wants "a clean modern aesthetic—nothing vintage or overly ornate—powered by bright, bold colours that jump off the screen and still look sharp in print"; asks for "a logo or word-mark element extracted from the main design so I can use it on merch and posters"; deliverables "supplied in layered PSD/AI, plus ready-to-post PNG and JPEG versions" and "scalable vector for print"; acceptance criteria "Design clearly reads at both large and thumbnail sizes" and "All fonts converted to outlines or included." REASONABLY ENRICHED into a runnable Adobe-only benchmark matching the slot (duotone gig poster, create-from-supplied-parts): the original brief implies from-scratch cover/logo art, which is NOT in connector scope (no from-scratch art/logo, no layout composition — document_render_layout/document_merge_data_layout only EXPORT/bind a GENUINE authored .indd). So the client is modeled as the fictional indie act "The Nocturne Sessions" supplying (a) an authored InDesign gig-poster template carrying real <<title>>/<<venue>>/<<date>>/<<support>>/<<presale>> merge fields plus a hero-photo frame and a wordmark frame, (b) a flat raster promo wordmark PNG to be vectorized for crisp large-format print, (c) one raw press photo of the band to be given the requested "bright, bold" two-tone (duotone) treatment so it reads at both poster and thumbnail size, and (d) a one-row CSV of the event copy. The Adobe chain does the genuinely-doable heavy lifting the brief rewards: prepping/duotoning the hero photo, vectorizing the wordmark, and binding the event copy through a real InDesign data-merge to export the print PDF + high-res JPEG. No deliverable invented beyond the poster/logo the brief asks for; the "doubles as a wordmark for posters/merch" ask is honored literally by the vectorize + poster placement.
one_line_ask: Give the raw band press photo a bold two-tone (duotone) FX treatment, vectorize the supplied Nocturne Sessions wordmark, then run the client's authored InDesign gig-poster template through a data-merge against the event-copy CSV to export a print-ready PDF and a high-res JPEG.
full_brief: The Nocturne Sessions, a fictional indie synth-noir act, is releasing a new EP and needs its launch gig poster finished for print and social. The band has handed off an authored InDesign poster template (nocturne_poster_template.indd), laid out A2 portrait with a full-bleed hero-photo frame, a wordmark frame, and real InDesign data-merge fields (<<title>>, <<venue>>, <<date>>, <<support>>, <<presale>>). They also supply one raw press photo of the trio (shot on a phone in a dim rehearsal room — slightly tilted, flat/dull, cool white balance, a cluttered gear-strewn background), a flat raster promo wordmark PNG that reads "THE NOCTURNE SESSIONS," and a one-row CSV holding the show copy. The band's brief calls for "a clean modern aesthetic—nothing vintage—powered by bright, bold colours that jump off the screen and still look sharp in print," and the poster's art direction is a classic bold two-tone (duotone) gig-poster look: the hero photo rendered in just two punchy brand inks (deep indigo shadows, hot magenta highlights) rather than full colour, so it reads instantly at both A2 wall size and Instagram thumbnail size. The job: (1) prep the raw press photo — straighten the tilted frame, auto-tone the flat exposure, and push contrast hard so the two-tone mapping has clean separation between shadow and highlight; (2) convert the prepped photo to a bold duotone — first flatten it to a single-ink monochromatic base tint in the indigo shadow ink across the whole frame, then lay the hot-magenta brand ink over it as a whole-image color overlay in a lighten/screen blend so only the brighter pixels shift toward magenta, producing the two-ink poster hero; add a fine grain so the flat inks don't band on large-format print; crop it to the poster frame's ratio; (3) vectorize the flat wordmark PNG into a clean SVG so the "NOCTURNE SESSIONS" word-mark stays razor-sharp at A2 and can be reused on merch/posters exactly as the brief asks; (4) recommend a modern condensed display font pairing for the show copy so the template's type reads as clean and bold; (5) upload the duotoned hero and the vector wordmark, then run the client's authored .indd through InDesign data-merge against the event-copy CSV so the title/venue/date/support/presale bind into the template around the placed hero and wordmark; (6) export the bound poster to a print-ready A2 PDF (300 DPI, CMYK-intent) and a high-resolution JPEG for social. All event copy stays exactly as supplied in the CSV. Deliverables: print-ready PDF, high-res JPEG, the duotone hero PNG, and the wordmark SVG.

inputs.name: nocturne_poster_template.indd
inputs.gen_prompt: AUTHORED DELIVERABLE (cannot be model-generated): a genuine Adobe InDesign document (.indd/.idml) for an A2 portrait gig poster in a bold modern two-tone style. Must contain one full-bleed hero-photo image frame, a wordmark image frame near the top, and REAL InDesign data-merge placeholder fields named <<title>>, <<venue>>, <<date>>, <<support>>, <<presale>> arranged in a clean typographic block down the lower third. Authored by hand in InDesign so the data-merge fields actually bind; a PDF-converted .indd with literal text would NOT bind.
inputs.realism_notes: Modeled as the band's own draft template (a normal freelancer revision/hand-off). document_merge_data_layout is [T] and REQUIRES a user-authored .indd carrying real merge fields — it cannot be connector-generated, so it is a supplied input, not a chain output.

inputs.name: band_press_photo_raw.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal phone snapshot of a three-piece indie synth band standing close together in a dim rehearsal room, amps and cables cluttered behind them, one member holding a synth. Slightly tilted horizon (about 3 degrees), flat dull under-lit exposure, cool/blue white balance, mixed room light, candid documentary look, real skin texture and fabric, no studio gloss, no CGI. Framed loosely with headroom so it can be cropped to a poster ratio.
inputs.realism_notes: Deliberately imperfect (tilt, flat/dull tone, cool WB, cluttered background) so the prep + high-contrast steps have real work to do before the two-tone mapping; gpt-image-2 for photoreal people/band.

inputs.name: nocturne_wordmark_flat.png
inputs.gen_prompt: A flat modern wordmark logo reading 'THE NOCTURNE SESSIONS' in a bold condensed sans-serif, solid cream-white letters on a transparent background, tight tracking, two stacked lines, high contrast, crisp flat raster PNG, no gradients, no glow, suitable for vectorizing.
inputs.realism_notes: gemini-3-pro-image for clean legible text/logo; flat high-contrast with hard edges so image_vectorize produces a clean SVG. This is the 'word-mark element for merch and posters' the brief asks for, supplied as flat art (not generated from scratch by the connector).

inputs.name: nocturne_event_copy.csv
inputs.gen_prompt: Generate a single-row CSV with columns title,venue,date,support,presale holding the show copy: title 'EP LAUNCH SHOW', venue 'The Glasshouse, Manchester', date 'FRI 12 SEP · DOORS 8PM', support 'w/ Violet Static + Low Hours', presale 'Presale Wed 10AM · nocturnesessions.live'. Column names must exactly match the InDesign merge fields <<title>>,<<venue>>,<<date>>,<<support>>,<<presale>>.
inputs.realism_notes: Single-row event copy so the merge binds the poster's fixed text block; fictional venue/support acts keep it IP-clean; column headers match the authored .indd merge fields so the [T] data-merge binds.

inputs.name: nocturne_brand_inks.txt
inputs.gen_prompt: A short brand note listing the two poster inks as hex/RGB values: shadow ink deep indigo #1B1A4B (RGB 27,26,75), highlight ink hot magenta #FF2E88 (RGB 255,46,136), plus a one-line instruction that the hero photo should read as indigo shadows and magenta highlights for the two-tone treatment.
inputs.realism_notes: Supplies the exact two brand colours the duotone steps target (monochromatic tint hue + color-overlay RGB), so the two-tone mapping is specified by the client rather than invented by the tool.

outputs.name: Nocturne_Poster_print.pdf
outputs.spec: Print-ready A2 portrait PDF exported from the data-merged InDesign document; event copy bound from CSV, duotoned hero photo placed in the full-bleed frame, vector wordmark placed; ~300 DPI, CMYK print intent. Also delivered as Nocturne_Poster_highres.jpg: High-resolution JPEG of the same bound poster (A2 portrait, max-quality) for Instagram/social sharing; must read at thumbnail size per the brief.

outputs.name: nocturne_hero_duotone.png
outputs.spec: The band press photo prepped (straightened, auto-toned, high-contrast) and rendered as a bold two-tone duotone — indigo shadows, hot-magenta highlights — with fine print grain, cropped to the poster frame ratio.

outputs.name: nocturne_wordmark.svg
outputs.spec: Clean vector SVG of the THE NOCTURNE SESSIONS wordmark for razor-sharp scaling at A2 and reuse on merch/posters.

connector_workflow.note: Preview the supplied raw press photo to confirm tilt, dull exposure and cluttered background before prepping.
Level the tilted horizon on the band press photo.
Auto-tone the straightened photo to fix the flat/dull under-lit exposure before the two-tone mapping.
Push contrast hard so the duotone mapping has clean shadow/highlight separation (deep shadows, bright highlights).

Flatten the whole high-contrast photo to a single-ink monochromatic base tint in the indigo shadow ink (hue from #1B1A4B in the brand note) — the standard duotone base ('make it duotone' is this tool's own use case); no mask, whole image.

Lay the hot-magenta highlight ink (RGB 255,46,136 from #FF2E88) over the WHOLE indigo base in a lighten blend so only the brighter pixels shift toward magenta — this produces the two-ink (indigo shadows / magenta highlights) duotone hero without any selection/mask (matches the tool's whole-image lighten/screen examples).
Add a fine grain so the flat two-tone inks don't band on large-format A2 print.
Crop/resize the duotone hero to the A2 poster hero-frame ratio the template expects.
Vectorize the flat wordmark PNG into a clean SVG so THE NOCTURNE SESSIONS stays razor-sharp at A2 and reusable on merch.
Recommend a modern condensed display + clean body pairing for the poster's title/venue/date copy so the type reads bold and clean (advisory, informs the template's fonts).
Initialize an upload session for the prepped poster assets (duotone hero, vector wordmark).
Finalize the upload; returns asset URNs the InDesign template/data-merge will reference for the hero frame and wordmark frame.

InDesign data-merge: bind the title/venue/date/support/presale row into the client's authored .indd (which carries real <<fields>>); the duotone hero + vector wordmark sit in their frames via additionalImageFiles. [T] requires the user-authored template.
Export the merged InDesign document to a print-ready ~300 DPI A2 PDF deliverable (CMYK print intent).
Export the same merged document to a high-resolution JPEG deliverable for social.

chaining_note: Strict output->input chaining throughout. The hero photo runs a 7-link prep+duotone pipeline: band_press_photo_raw.jpg -> preview(1) [inspection] and -> auto_straighten(2) -> auto_tone(3) -> brightness_and_contrast(4) -> monochromatic_tint(5, whole-image indigo base) -> color_overlay(6, whole-image magenta in a lighten blend so only brighter pixels shift) -> add_grain(7) -> crop_and_resize(8) = nocturne_hero_duotone.png, each step consuming only the prior step's output. The two-ink look is built entirely whole-image — no selection/mask step — so the duotone emerges purely from tint(5) + blended overlay(6) as those two tools' own documented use cases describe. The wordmark chains its raw PNG -> vectorize(9). font_recommend(10) reads the event-copy CSV to inform the template type (advisory, no downstream binding). Both prepped outputs (nocturne_hero_duotone.png, nocturne_wordmark.svg) converge into the upload session(11) -> finalize(12) -> data-merge(13), which also consumes the authored nocturne_poster_template.indd + event-copy CSV. The merged .indd(13) is the single source for both exports — render PDF(14) and render JPEG(15). Nothing after the raw input loads originates outside the chain; the final poster emerges only from the assembled chain.
difficulty_rationale: Long-horizon flagship: 15 connector calls, 14 distinct Adobe tools across 4 groups (image_* raster, asset_* storage/preview, document_* InDesign, font_recommend) — above the >=10 calls / >=6 distinct-tool floor. Difficulty comes from constructing a genuine two-tone (duotone) hero without any banned capability: the two-ink look is built by real whole-image steps (high-contrast prep -> monochromatic base tint in ink 1 -> whole-image color-overlay of ink 2 in a lighten/screen blend so only the brighter pixels shift -> grain), not by a text-to-image or generative-fill shortcut and not by any tonal-range selection the tools do not support. It then vectorizes a supplied flat wordmark, converges everything into a genuine InDesign [T] data-merge, and dual-exports. It respects every hard limit: no generative fill / generative-expand / reframe-crop / object-removal-with-reconstruction / text-to-image / from-scratch art or logo / upscaling; image_fill_area and image_select_by_prompt are unused; the layout is NOT composed by the connector — document_render_layout / document_merge_data_layout only EXPORT/bind the band's authored .indd, which is a supplied input; the wordmark is supplied flat art and only vectorized (not designed from scratch); all event copy stays exactly as supplied in the CSV per the brief.

reverify.banned_capability_check: None of the banned capabilities are used. No image_crop_and_resize / reframe-crop / reframe-crop; no generative fill of new content; no text-to-image or from-scratch art/logo (the wordmark is a SUPPLIED flat PNG that is only vectorized; the duotone hero is a SUPPLIED photo transformed by tone/tint/overlay/grain); no upscaling / super-resolution; no object-removal-with-reconstruction / inpainting (no removal step at all — the cluttered background is left in and simply rendered in two inks); no bespoke font/letterform design (font_recommend only suggests existing fonts); no unreachable external accounts. The prior draft's image_select_by_prompt step (used to 'select the bright/light portions') was REMOVED: that tool selects only non-anatomical, uniquely-identifiable objects/scene-elements or named body parts and explicitly forbids abstract/negative-space prompts, so it cannot select a tonal/luminance range — using it that way was out of bounds. The two-ink mapping is now achieved whole-image via image_apply_monochromatic_tint (whose documented use case literally includes 'make it duotone') plus image_apply_color_overlay in a lighten blend (its own examples are whole-image lighten/screen/overlay), which is fully in-bounds.
reverify.notes: Each potentially-risky step stays in-bounds: (1) The duotone is built from real palette primitives, whole-image, with NO selection: image_apply_monochromatic_tint flattens to one ink (indigo hue), then image_apply_color_overlay lays the second ink (magenta RGB) in a lighten blend so only the brighter pixels shift toward magenta — both tools are listed in the palette and both operate whole-image here (matching their own documented examples), so no unsupported tonal-selection is claimed. (2) image_add_grain and image_adjust_brightness_and_contrast are palette raster adjusters used whole-image. (3) image_crop_and_resize is a straight crop to a ratio, no generative expand. (4) image_vectorize converts SUPPLIED flat raster to SVG — allowed. (5) document_merge_data_layout is [T] and requires the client's AUTHORED .indd with real <<fields>> (declared as a supplied input, kind:data, gen_model:'n/a-authored-asset'); the CSV headers exactly match the merge fields, the duotone hero + wordmark bind via additionalImageFiles, and the spec flags that a PDF-converted .indd would not bind — so no layout is composed by the connector. (6) document_render_layout only EXPORTS the merged authored .indd (PDF + JPEG). (7) asset_preview_file is a read-only inspection. No Express-widget tools (search_design/fill_text/animate_design/change_background_color) and no Canva tools are present; all steps server=='adobe'. workflow_nature=create is satisfied: a NEW poster deliverable is assembled from SUPPLIED parts (authored template, supplied photo, supplied wordmark, supplied copy) via non-generative steps. tool_call_count=15 equals the number of connector_workflow steps; distinct_adobe_tools=14 (all 14 names in tools_used are unique and adobe-server).
